channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: How watching Aussies win Olympic gold 'changed everything' for rugby star
item.description: When Isabella Nasser tuned in to watch the 2016 Rio Olympics as a 13-year-old, she had little idea of the pathway her life was about to take.
item.dc:creator: Luke Pentony

media:group.media:description: Australian co-captain Isabella Nasser was inspired to play rugby sevens as a teenager.

item.title: Is Canberra's CBD unsafe? It depends who you ask
item.description: ACT industry groups say drug use and widespread construction are among issues eroding feelings of safety.
item.dc:creator: Lily Nothling

media:group.media:description: Canberrans have mixed perceptions when it comes to safety in Civic with some fearful of anti-social behaviour.

item.title: Child traumatised by family violence now learning 'new words every day'
item.description: When Claire arrived at a women's refuge in outer Sydney with her four-year-old daughter in tow, the child was "barely able to speak clearly".
item.dc:creator: Isabella Ross

media:group.media:description: Claire is thankful for the support of a "guardian angel" caseworker for helping her child.

item.title: X fights Andrew and Tristan Tate's bid to unmask anonymous critics
item.description: Misogynist influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate have struck a hurdle in their bid to unmask anonymous users they allege have defamed them, with social media platform X asking a Florida court to dismiss the motion.
item.dc:creator: Jordanna Schriever

media:group.media:description: Andrew Tate, left, and his brother Tristan, want X to reveal the identity of several anonymous defendants they allege have defamed them.

item.title: Victorian town braces for impact of impending Bega Cheese factory closure
item.description: Bega Cheese's Strathmerton factory, which employed more than 300 workers, is set to close its doors on June 30, leaving residents in the small Victorian town wondering what's next.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Mangelsdorf

media:group.media:description: Van Bui has mixed feelings about the factory closure but remains hopeful about the town's future.

item.title: Photographer accused of stealing Bondi terror attack victim's camera
item.description: A photographer who allegedly stole a camera from a Bondi terror attack victim before pawning it days later has been charged.

media:group.media:description: A photographer who allegedly stole a Bondi terror attack victim's camera has been charged.

item.title: Pendlebury's 433rd game simply his latest of many AFL records
item.description: Scott Pendlebury is set to be the proud holder of the AFL's games record, but it's only the latest in a long line of records held by the Collingwood icon.
item.dc:creator: Cody Atkinson and Sean Lawson

media:group.media:description: Scott Pendlebury is a proud owner of several AFL records that are less heralded than his 433 career games and counting.

item.title: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's image has been revealed. Here's why
item.description: The youngest of the Snowtown killers was the subject of a long-running suppression order that kept his face — or any description of his appearance — hidden for 25 years. Here's why that's been lifted.
item.dc:creator: Jordanna Schriever

media:group.media:description: James Vlassakis was one of four perpetrators in the Snowtown serial killings.

item.title: A year on, survivors still counting cost of 'one-in-500-year' disaster
item.description: It was six months before Ed Trotter could return home after record floods on the NSW Mis North Coast. A year on from the disaster, others are still without working kitchens and bathrooms.
item.dc:creator: Emma Siossian and Wiriya Sati

media:group.media:description: Ed Trotter has found comfort restoring his home and garden after the record-breaking May 2025 flood.

item.title: Man charged with kidnapping Marvin Oraiha months before his alleged murder
item.description: A man is charged over the alleged kidnapping of a low-level bikie associate who was shot dead in Sydney's west three years ago.

media:group.media:description: A 27-year-old man has been charged over the alleged kidnapping of Marvin Oraiha.

item.title: Recycling scheme contractor accused of having employees sleep on depot floor sues for $5m
item.description: Allegations that a depot operator in Queensland's container exchange recycling program conducted itself in a manner that could be likened to modern slavery have been detailed during a court hearing in a multi-million dollar federal court dispute.
item.dc:creator: Liana Walker

media:group.media:description: Cash refund point operator in Queensland's container recycling scheme, U Can Recycle, has taken COEX to court.

item.title: Aged care refunds for overcharging a 'burden' and 'total joke,' advocates say
item.description: Some advocates say the government's decision to delay price caps for at-home aged care services is a "burden".

media:group.media:description: Peter Willcocks says he believed providers would "come up with a spin" to justify their charges.

item.title: Meet the staff and volunteers who make Meals on Wheels go round
item.description: For 70 years, Meals on Wheels has been serving Ipswich residents, and every meal comes with a welfare check and a conversation.
item.dc:creator: Jessica van Vonderen

media:group.media:description: Retired teachers Sam and Glenda Carroll get a hot lunch delivered by Meals on Wheels in Ipswich most days.

item.title: 'Survival mode all the time': Australian parents break taboos on raising kids
item.description: Almost 60 per cent of Australian parents are experiencing psychological distress, according to the largest-ever national survey of parents as the birth rate hits a record low
item.dc:creator: Conor Duffy and Rhiannon Hobbins

media:group.media:description: Catherine Wade says parents across all demographics are struggling.

item.title: WHO-linked report warns world more at risk of pandemic now than before COVID
item.description: As health leaders face deadly outbreaks of Ebola and hantavirus, a major report finds the world's response system remains dangerously underprepared.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Courty

media:group.media:description: Infectious diseases are becoming more frequent and damaging, a major report has found.

item.title: Trawlers may sit idle but residents are holding onto Karumba's future
item.description: Karumba's rich fishing industry dates back to the roaring, prawning era of the 60s, but major blows are seeing a seafood decline. However, the Gulf of Carpentaria residents say they "wouldn't live anywhere else".
item.dc:creator: Abbey Halter

media:group.media:description: Karumba is in the mouth of the Gulf of Carpentaria, known for its seafood industry and rowdy local pub.

item.title: Trump's pick for ambassador to Australia names three priorities for role
item.description: David Brat, who rose to political prominence as part of the conservative Tea Party movement, takes questions from senators at a confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Brad Ryan

media:group.media:description: David Brat was asked about AUKUS, Ukraine and critical minerals at the Senate confirmation hearing.

item.title: They’re black, white and very cute. But not all gentoo penguins are the same
item.description: Gentoo penguins were thought to be a standalone species, but a new study says there are four distinct species — and it could lead to better conservation plans.
item.dc:creator: Jano Gibson

media:group.media:description: Gentoo penguins can be distinguished by their bright red-orange bills and conspicuous white eye patches.

item.title: 'Short-term pain' to wrestle Tasmanian budget back to black in two years
item.description: There will be "short-term pain" to get Tasmania's finances back in the black within two years, state treasurer Eric Abetz says ahead of today's budget.
item.dc:creator: Josh Duggan and Lucy MacDonald

media:group.media:description: Tasmanian Treasurer Eric Abetz released his first "interim" state budget on the 6th of November 2025. Hobart, Tasmania.

item.title: Food waste from Queen Victoria Market to help vulnerable Melburnians
item.description: A new program at Melbourne's Queen Victoria Market is tackling the massive amount of food wasted, while helping the rising number of people struggling to put food on the table.
item.dc:creator: Ethan White and Mikaela Ortolan

media:group.media:description: Muz Ozdemir sorts through his produce and collects quality items to donate.

item.title: Can Australia make its own fertiliser without fossil fuels?
item.description: Most fertiliser is a by-product of the oil industry, which is why it's so vulnerable to the current blockade. But there are other sources the industry could eventually switch to.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Phiddian

media:group.media:description: Fertilisers are made with fossil fuels, but the current shortage is driving interest in more sustainable sources.

item.title: Labor burns political capital as tax reform backlash grows
item.description: In an already difficult post-budget week, Labor isn't being helped by some of its "friends". But it has won an important endorsement from one powerful voice.
item.dc:creator: David Speers

media:group.media:description: Labor has not looked well-prepared for where the post-budget debate has gone.

item.title: El Niño looms as BOM releases long-range winter forecast
item.description: The Bureau of Meteorology is forecasting winter to be unusually warm across the entire country as an El Niño climate pattern is increasingly likely to take hold.
item.dc:creator: Tyne Logan

media:group.media:description: Rainfall for the 3-month period from June to August is likely to be below average for most of the country.

item.title: WA gas users warn Labor not to repeat flaws in state reservation scheme
item.description: Western Australian gas users are urging the Albanese government to build stronger transparency and enforcement into its proposed national gas reserve scheme.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Labor has said the policy addresses forecast gas shortfalls on the east coast and aims to lower prices for the domestic market.

item.title: ‘We're not fleeing’: The disappearing Pacific nation relocating its people to Australia
item.description: Tuvalu is building new land to hold back rising sea levels, but the majority of its people are applying to relocate to Australia. As they do, many are confronting a disturbing question.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Fennell, Alice Lolohea, Cordelia Brown, Steven Viney, and Sean Mantesso

media:group.media:description: An aerial photo of land reclamation work in Tuvalu's capital Funafuti.
